reasoning: Elon Musk recently had his massive compensation package at Tesla re-approved and is heavily focused on Tesla's pivot towards autonomous driving and AI, including Robotaxi plans rolling out in 2025/2026. While he is taking on an advisory role in the Trump administration (Department of Government Efficiency), this is not expected to require him to step down from his corporate roles. Musk's central vision is deeply tied to being CEO of Tesla to see these projects through. Although unforeseen circumstances (scandal, health, abrupt shifting of focus to xAI/SpaceX) pose a baseline risk, the probability of him stepping down voluntarily or being ousted in 2025 remains quite low.
